name: oh-my-claudecode
description: Throughput, autonomy & DEFAULT-coding lane. This is the DEFAULT work-style lane for general code work — feature implementation, solo bug-fixing, root-cause tracing, refactors, and step-by-step planning all land here UNLESS the operator explicitly asks for superpowers' structural discipline gate (per-task spec+quality reviewer, test-first, or a verification/peer-review gate). Route here when the work is about general authoring, SCALE, or RUNNING ON ITS OWN rather than an explicitly-requested correctness gate: many independent edits at once, workers coordinating on a shared task list, hands-off lifecycle from a short brief, looping until tests pass, read-only investigation with no fix — both 'why did this happen' causal tracing (trace) AND 'how is this codebase/repo built' deep structural analysis (sciomc), light scoping, or research/knowledge ops. WITHIN this lane, actively prefer the `team` skill (over ultrawork/autopilot) the moment the work needs ANY of: (a) inter-worker communication or a shared task list, (b) a separate review/specialist role in the loop (architect/critic/verifier/security review), (c) each worker isolated on its OWN branch while a lead owns plan/review/merge, or (d) the controlling session must stay clean — delegate edits to workers, keep main for planning/review/merge, and spin workers up or down as the work shifts. `team` is the default for multi-worker delegated work with a coordinating lead; reserve `ultrawork` for bounded parallel edits that need NO cross-worker comms/review (e.g. rename X across 20 files), and `autopilot` for hands-off single-track lifecycle. It governs HOW you work — NOT a document/slide/image domain (those are installed skills). Escalate to superpowers ONLY when the operator EXPLICITLY requests a per-task spec+quality reviewer, test-first authoring, or a verification/peer-review gate — NOT merely because the work touches code, fixes a bug, or makes a plan (those default here).

skills.name: throughput-autonomy-lane
skills.description: Representative signals that route to oh-my-claudecode (the session then picks the exact OMC skill).
examples: rename X across 20 files / many independent edits at once
여러 파일 한꺼번에 싹 다 바꿔줘
workers coordinate on a shared task list (multi-role review)
delegate edits to workers on isolated branches; lead keeps main for plan/review/merge; add or shut down workers as needed (team)
main 컨텍스트는 깨끗하게 두고 수정은 워커 브랜치에 위임, merge만 main에서 (team)
from a 2-3 line idea, run the whole lifecycle hands-off
keep looping on its own until tests pass
explain WHY this happened — causal analysis only, not fixing (trace)
성능이 왜 느린지 분석만, 고치진 말고
analyze how this repo/architecture is built — read-only structural deep-dive (sciomc)
이 repo 코드 구조/아키텍처가 어떻게 동작하는지 분석해줘 (sciomc, 고치진 말고)
clarify my requirements before building / scope this lightly
